channel.title: Department of Justice - Antitrust Division Press Releases
channel.description: Press releases from the DOJ Antitrust Division

item.title: Justice Department Sues Google for Monopolizing Digital Advertising Technologies
item.description: The Justice Department filed an antitrust lawsuit against Google (NASDAQ: GOOGL), alleging the company monopolized key digital advertising technologies through anticompetitive acquisitions.

item.title: Justice Department Blocks Merger of Kroger and Albertsons
item.description: The Antitrust Division moved to block the proposed $24.6 billion merger of Kroger (NYSE: KR) and Albertsons, citing harm to consumers through reduced competition in the grocery industry.

item.title: Major Technology Company Settles Antitrust Investigation for $2.3 Billion
item.description: A major technology company has agreed to pay $2.3 billion to settle antitrust claims, the largest such settlement in the division's history.

item.title: Justice Department Opens Investigation into Airline Industry Pricing Practices
item.description: The Antitrust Division announced it has opened a probe into potential price-fixing among major US airlines, including Delta (NYSE: DAL) and United Airlines (NASDAQ: UAL).

item.title: Consent Decree Approved in Hospital Merger Case
item.description: A federal court approved a consent decree requiring divestitures in the merger of two regional hospital systems, addressing competition concerns in three metropolitan areas.
